title: ampacus micrococca
intro: Ampacus micrococca, also known as the micrococca plant or the poison nut, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Ampacus micrococca include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-parasitic agent. Herbal preparations of Ampacus micrococca can be made by infusing the leaves in water or mixing them with other herbs to create a tea or tincture for medicinal use.
